name: Joachim Stutschewsky
abstract: Joachim-Yehoyachin Stutschewsky (Hebrew: יהויכין סטוצ'בסקי, Russian: Иоахим Стучевский; 7 February 1891, Romny, Russian Empire  – 14 November 1982, Tel Aviv, Israel) was a Ukraine-born and Israeli cellist, composer, musicologist.

concerts.label: Julia Stutschewsky, Soprano, and Joachim Stutschewsky, Cello

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: I remember we rented it out to a cellist who was quite famous. He was a member of something called the Kolisch Quartet... His name was Joachim Stutschewsky. He was a very nasty man who didn’t like children. He was not very nice to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
